result.stdout:
Description:
  Semtex - Remove the Git friction that is discouraging you from making improvements to your C# codebase

Usage:
  Semtex [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  split                                                   Partition uncommitted changes into two patch files: one for behavioural changes and one for quality improvements.
  split-remote <repo-url> <target>                        Split a specific commit from a remote repository into two patch files: one for behavioural changes and one for quality improvements.
  check <repo-url> <target>                               Analyze the specified remote repository and branch/commit to identify changes that will affect the program's runtime behaviour.
  commit <Behavioural|Quality> <message>                  Commits one of the partitions of your changes generated by the split commands
  compute-project-file-map <sln-path> <output-file-path>  This command is a prerequisite if your codebase does not follow the standard hierarchical project layout. It generates a file that maps each C# file to its respective project.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Semtex - Remove the Git friction that is discouraging you from making improvements to your C# codebase

Usage:
  Semtex [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  split                                                   Partition uncommitted changes into two patch files: one for behavioural changes and one for quality improvements.
  split-remote <repo-url> <target>                        Split a specific commit from a remote repository into two patch files: one for behavioural changes and one for quality improvements.
  check <repo-url> <target>                               Analyze the specified remote repository and branch/commit to identify changes that will affect the program's runtime behaviour.
  commit <Behavioural|Quality> <message>                  Commits one of the partitions of your changes generated by the split commands
  compute-project-file-map <sln-path> <output-file-path>  This command is a prerequisite if your codebase does not follow the standard hierarchical project layout. It generates a file that maps each C# file to its respective project.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Analyze the specified remote repository and branch/commit to identify changes that will affect the program's runtime behaviour.

Usage:
  Semtex check <repo-url> <target> [options]

Arguments:
  <repo-url>  Origin url of Git repository, including protocol (https, ssh, etc.).
  <target>    Branch or SHA containing the changes.

Options:
  --base <base>                                                      Specify the base branch or SHA for comparison. The default value is the parent commit of target.
  --all-ancestors                                                    Analyze the previous 250 ancestors of the given commit. If this option is used, --base is ignored. [default: False]
  --project-filter <project-filter>                                  Only consider changes in a specific project. Useful for debugging.
  --project-map-file <project-map-file>                              Use this option if your codebase does not follow the standard hierarchical project layout. Refer to the compute-project-file-map command for generating this file.
  --verbosity <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  Set the logging verbosity level. [default: Information]
  -?, -h, --help                                                     Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Analyze the specified remote repository and branch/commit to identify changes that will affect the program's runtime behaviour.

Usage:
  Semtex check <repo-url> <target> [options]

Arguments:
  <repo-url>  Origin url of Git repository, including protocol (https, ssh, etc.).
  <target>    Branch or SHA containing the changes.

Options:
  --base <base>                                                      Specify the base branch or SHA for comparison. The default value is the parent commit of target.
  --all-ancestors                                                    Analyze the previous 250 ancestors of the given commit. If this option is used, --base is ignored. [default: False]
  --project-filter <project-filter>                                  Only consider changes in a specific project. Useful for debugging.
  --project-map-file <project-map-file>                              Use this option if your codebase does not follow the standard hierarchical project layout. Refer to the compute-project-file-map command for generating this file.
  --verbosity <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  Set the logging verbosity level. [default: Information]
  -?, -h, --help                                                     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Commits one of the partitions of your changes generated by the split commands

Usage:
  Semtex commit <type> <message> [options]

Arguments:
  <Behavioural|Quality>  Whether to commit behavioural changes or quality improvements.
  <message>              Commit message

Options:
  --repo-path <repo-path>                                            Path to the local Git repository checkout. Accepts both absolute and relative paths. [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-semtex-1.1.2-e34beaebac2f4afdbce4919474ff644a]
  --verbosity <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  Set the logging verbosity level. [default: Information]
  -?, -h, --help                                                     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  This command is a prerequisite if your codebase does not follow the standard hierarchical project layout. It generates a file that maps each C# file to its respective project.

Usage:
  Semtex compute-project-file-map <sln-path> <output-file-path> [options]

Arguments:
  <sln-path>          Path to local solution file. Accepts both absolute and relative paths.
  <output-file-path>  Defines the path where the mapping will be saved.

Options:
  --verbosity <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  Set the logging verbosity level. [default: Information]
  -?, -h, --help                                                     Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  This command is a prerequisite if your codebase does not follow the standard hierarchical project layout. It generates a file that maps each C# file to its respective project.

Usage:

Arguments:

  <output-file-path>  Defines the path where the mapping will be saved.

Options:
  --verbosity <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  Set the logging verbosity level. [default: Information]
  -?, -h, --help                                                     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Partition uncommitted changes into two patch files: one for behavioural changes and one for quality improvements.

Usage:
  Semtex split [options]

Options:
  --repo-path <repo-path>                                            Path to the local Git repository checkout. Accepts both absolute and relative paths. [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-semtex-1.1.2-e34beaebac2f4afdbce4919474ff644a]
  --base <base>                                                      Branch or SHA to compare against. [default: HEAD]
  --include-uncommitted <None|Staged|Unstaged>                       Specify which uncommitted changes to include. [default: Staged]
  --verbosity <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  Set the logging verbosity level. [default: Information]
  --project-map-file <project-map-file>                              Use this option if your codebase does not follow the standard hierarchical project layout. Refer to the compute-project-file-map command for generating this file.
  -?, -h, --help                                                     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Split a specific commit from a remote repository into two patch files: one for behavioural changes and one for quality improvements.

Usage:
  Semtex split-remote <repo-url> <target> [options]

Arguments:
  <repo-url>  Origin url of Git repository, including protocol (https, ssh, etc.).
  <target>    Branch or SHA containing the changes you want to split.

Options:
  --base <base>                                                      Specify the base branch or SHA for comparison. The default value is the parent commit of target.
  --verbosity <Critical|Debug|Error|Information|None|Trace|Warning>  Set the logging verbosity level [default: Information]
  --project-map-file <project-map-file>                              Use this option if your codebase does not follow the standard hierarchical project layout. Refer to the compute-project-file-map command for generating this file.
  -?, -h, --help                                                     Show help and usage information
